hero_subtitle: A credential security education tool. 100% in your browser. No data sent. No tracking.
m1_desc: Understand what makes your password weak or strong. Real feedback, not just a score.
m1_empty_hint: Type a password to analyze it
m2_desc: Has your password been leaked? We check using k-anonymity: the server never sees your password.
m2_empty_hint: Enter a password to check if it has been leaked
m2_result_safe_detail: This password does not appear in any known compromised credentials database analyzed by HaveIBeenPwned.
m2_result_found_detail: This password was exposed in known data breaches. If you use it anywhere, change it now.
m2_error_api: The API returned an error ({code}). Please try again.
m2_error_network: Network error. Check your connection and try again.
m2_kanon_toggle: How do we check without sending your password?
m2_step1_desc: Your browser computes the SHA-1 hash of your password locally. The hash never leaves your device.
m2_step2_desc: Only the first 5 chars of the hash are sent. Not enough to reconstruct your password.
m2_step3_desc: The API returns ~500–1000 hashes sharing that prefix. Your password is anonymous among hundreds.
m2_step4_desc: Your browser compares locally. The server never knew which password you were checking.
m2_kanon_footer: The server only sees "AAAAA". Your password stays anonymous among hundreds of hashes. That's k-anonymity.
m3_desc: Generate truly secure passphrases using the EFF Diceware method.
m3_hint: Generate your first secure passphrase using the EFF Diceware method.
m4_desc: How long would it take an attacker to crack your password? Three real-world scenarios.
m4_hint: Type a password to see how long it takes to crack based on the hashing backend.
m4_sc_online_desc: Rate-limited web login. 10 attempts per second.
m4_sc_bcrypt_desc: Modern hash backend. Offline GPU against a leaked database.
m4_sc_md5_desc: Legacy hash backend. 8× RTX 4090 rig against leaked hashes.
m4_explainer: The same password can take centuries or minutes to crack depending on how the service stores your hash. That's why it matters to choose services that use bcrypt, scrypt or argon2 — not MD5 or unsalted SHA-1.
footer_sys: [SYS] no data was transmitted. local session.
disclaimer_wip: work in progress · use with discretion
